# SC-12: SAST

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Static Application Security Testing: analyse the full codebase for security vulnerabilities mapped to CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) categories. Apply heightened scrutiny to agent-generated code. Block release of any critical or high-severity findings.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 4 | Testing & Documentation | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Execute static analysis scan across full codebase; map findings to CWE; generate findings report. |
| **Human does** | Review findings; triage; remediate or accept residual risk per severity policy. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| SAST scan report | evidence | Complete static analysis findings report with CWE mappings, severity ratings, and remediation recommendations. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_release |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | SAST tool (language-specific) |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

Critical and high-severity findings block Stage 4 exit. Must be remediated or have formal risk acceptance.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 24 | General requirements for ICT testing including security testing |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(2) | Examination of AI systems for biases and cybersecurity vulnerabilities |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
